scenario: After completing a procedure, a dental nurse mentions that a patient complains about waiting times. You consider what the most appropriate response would be.

options.A: Discuss the situation with your educational supervisor for guidance
options.B: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved
options.C: Prioritise immediate patient safety and assess the situation
options.D: Respond defensively to the concern
options.E: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved

explanation:
This scenario assesses seeking supervision appropriately. The most
appropriate responses prioritise patient safety, professional
behaviour, and effective communication. Seeking guidance from
supervisors, documenting concerns, and reflecting on practice
demonstrate responsibility and commitment to professional development.
In contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming
colleagues can undermine teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: While working in a hospital department, you become aware that you feel uncertain about performing a procedure. Consider the most appropriate way to respond as a Dental Core Trainee.

options.A: Communicate clearly with those involved
options.B: Blame another colleague
options.C: Ignore the issue
options.D: Document the situation appropriately
options.E: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected
options.F: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected
options.G: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern
options.H: Seek advice from a senior colleague

explanation:
This scenario assesses handling feedback. The most appropriate
responses prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and
effective communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors,
documenting concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate
responsibility and commitment to professional development. In
contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming
colleagues can undermine teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: During a busy clinic session, a patient tells you that there may have been a documentation error in the patient record. You must decide how best to respond while maintaining professionalism and ensuring patient safety.

options.A: Reflect on your own practice and identify improvements
options.B: Blame another colleague without investigation
options.C: Document the concern appropriately
options.D: Discuss the situation with your educational supervisor for guidance
options.E: Reflect on your own practice and identify improvements

explanation:
This scenario assesses patient-centred care. The most appropriate

effective communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors,

responsibility and commitment to professional development. In
contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming

prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and effective
communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors, documenting
concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate responsibility and
commitment to professional development. In contrast, ignoring
concerns, responding defensively, or blaming colleagues can undermine
teamwork and may compromise patient care.

options.A: Document the situation appropriately
options.B: Communicate clearly with those involved
options.C: Communicate clearly with those involved
options.D: Dismiss the concern without discussion
options.E: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern
options.G: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected
options.H: Seek advice from a senior colleague

options.B: Prioritise immediate patient safety and assess the situation
options.C: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved
options.D: Document the concern appropriately
options.E: Ignore the issue and continue working as normal

explanation:
This scenario assesses patient-centred care. The most appropriate
responses prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and
effective communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors,
documenting concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate
responsibility and commitment to professional development. In
contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming
colleagues can undermine teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: While working in a hospital department, you become aware that you feel uncertain about performing a procedure. Consider the most appropriate way to respond as a Dental Core Trainee.

options.A: Document the situation appropriately
options.B: Ignore the issue
options.C: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected
options.D: Blame another colleague
options.E: Seek advice from a senior colleague
options.F: Seek advice from a senior colleague
options.G: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern

responses prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and
effective communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors,
documenting concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate
responsibility and commitment to professional development. In
contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming
colleagues can undermine teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: You are reviewing patient notes when you realise that a patient appears distressed after a procedure. You must decide what action to take.

options.A: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved
options.B: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved
options.C: Document the concern appropriately
options.D: Prioritise immediate patient safety and assess the situation
options.E: Ignore the issue and continue working as normal

scenario: While working in a hospital department, you become aware that a concern has been raised about your communication with patients. Consider the most appropriate way to respond as a Dental Core Trainee.

options.A: Seek advice from a senior colleague
options.B: Document the situation appropriately
options.C: Dismiss the concern without discussion
options.D: Blame another colleague

options.G: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected

options.B: Seek advice from a senior colleague
options.C: Seek advice from a senior colleague
options.D: Communicate clearly with those involved
options.E: Communicate clearly with those involved
options.G: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern
options.H: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected

explanation:
This scenario assesses seeking supervision appropriately. The most
appropriate responses prioritise patient safety, professional
behaviour, and effective communication. Seeking guidance from
supervisors, documenting concerns, and reflecting on practice
demonstrate responsibility and commitment to professional development.
In contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming
colleagues can undermine teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: While working in a hospital department, you become aware that a patient complains about waiting times. Consider the most appropriate way to respond as a Dental Core Trainee.

options.A: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved
options.B: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved
options.C: Reflect on your own practice and identify improvements
options.D: Discuss the situation with your educational supervisor for guidance
options.E: Blame another colleague without investigation

options.C: Blame another colleague
options.D: Ignore the issue
options.E: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern

responses prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and
effective communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors,
documenting concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate
responsibility and commitment to professional development. In
contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming
colleagues can undermine teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: During a busy clinic session, a colleague tells you that there may have been a documentation error in the patient record. You must decide how best to respond while maintaining professionalism and ensuring patient safety.

options.A: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected
options.B: Seek advice from a senior colleague
options.C: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected
options.D: Dismiss the concern without discussion
options.E: Document the situation appropriately
options.H: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern

scenario: You are reviewing patient notes when you realise that a colleague may not be following infection control procedures. You must decide what action to take.

options.A: Document the concern appropriately
options.B: Discuss the situation with your educational supervisor for guidance
options.C: Ignore the issue and continue working as normal
options.D: Prioritise immediate patient safety and assess the situation
options.E: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved

prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and effective
communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors, documenting
concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate responsibility and
commitment to professional development. In contrast, ignoring
concerns, responding defensively, or blaming colleagues can undermine
teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: During a busy clinic session, a receptionist tells you that a colleague may not be following infection control procedures. You must decide how best to respond while maintaining professionalism and ensuring patient safety.

options.A: Discuss the situation with your educational supervisor for guidance
options.B: Reflect on your own practice and identify improvements
options.C: Blame another colleague without investigation
options.D: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved
options.E: Reflect on your own practice and identify improvements

scenario: While working in a hospital department, you become aware that a colleague asks you to sign documentation you did not complete. Consider the most appropriate way to respond as a Dental Core Trainee.

options.A: Dismiss the concern without discussion
options.B: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern
options.C: Seek advice from a senior colleague
options.D: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected
options.E: Document the situation appropriately
options.H: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern

options.B: Discuss the situation with your educational supervisor for guidance
options.C: Prioritise immediate patient safety and assess the situation
options.D: Reflect on your own practice and identify improvements
options.E: Blame another colleague without investigation

explanation:
This scenario assesses coping with pressure. The most appropriate
responses prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and
effective communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors,
documenting concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate
responsibility and commitment to professional development. In
contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming
colleagues can undermine teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: After completing a procedure, your educational supervisor mentions that a patient complains about waiting times. You consider what the most appropriate response would be.

options.A: Communicate clearly with those involved
options.B: Seek advice from a senior colleague
options.C: Ignore the issue
options.D: Communicate clearly with those involved
options.E: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern
options.G: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern
options.H: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected

explanation:
This scenario assesses seeking supervision appropriately. The most
appropriate responses prioritise patient safety, professional
behaviour, and effective communication. Seeking guidance from
supervisors, documenting concerns, and reflecting on practice
demonstrate responsibility and commitment to professional development.
In contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming
colleagues can undermine teamwork and may compromise patient care.

options.E: Prioritise immediate patient safety and assess the situation

This scenario assesses clinical safety. The most appropriate responses
prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and effective
communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors, documenting
concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate responsibility and
commitment to professional development. In contrast, ignoring
concerns, responding defensively, or blaming colleagues can undermine
teamwork and may compromise patient care.

explanation:
This scenario assesses team communication. The most appropriate
responses prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and
effective communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors,
documenting concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate
responsibility and commitment to professional development. In
contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming
colleagues can undermine teamwork and may compromise patient care.

options.B: Discuss the situation with your educational supervisor for guidance
options.C: Respond defensively to the concern
options.D: Reflect on your own practice and identify improvements
options.E: Document the concern appropriately

explanation:
This scenario assesses professionalism. The most appropriate responses
prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and effective
communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors, documenting
concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate responsibility and
commitment to professional development. In contrast, ignoring
concerns, responding defensively, or blaming colleagues can undermine
teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: During a busy clinic session, a patient tells you that a colleague asks you to sign documentation you did not complete. You must decide how best to respond while maintaining professionalism and ensuring patient safety.

options.A: Blame another colleague
options.B: Communicate clearly with those involved
options.C: Dismiss the concern without discussion
options.D: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern
options.E: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected

options.B: Seek advice from a senior colleague
options.C: Ignore the issue
options.D: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected
options.E: Dismiss the concern without discussion
options.H: Seek advice from a senior colleague

explanation:
This scenario assesses seeking supervision appropriately. The most
appropriate responses prioritise patient safety, professional
behaviour, and effective communication. Seeking guidance from
supervisors, documenting concerns, and reflecting on practice
demonstrate responsibility and commitment to professional development.
In contrast, ignoring concerns, responding defensively, or blaming
colleagues can undermine teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: You are reviewing patient notes when you realise that a patient appears distressed after a procedure. You must decide what action to take.

options.A: Reflect on your own practice and identify improvements
options.B: Document the concern appropriately
options.C: Document the concern appropriately
options.D: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved
options.E: Respond defensively to the concern

explanation:
This scenario assesses professionalism. The most appropriate responses
prioritise patient safety, professional behaviour, and effective
communication. Seeking guidance from supervisors, documenting
concerns, and reflecting on practice demonstrate responsibility and
commitment to professional development. In contrast, ignoring
concerns, responding defensively, or blaming colleagues can undermine
teamwork and may compromise patient care.

scenario: While working in a hospital department, you become aware that a patient becomes upset about the outcome of treatment. Consider the most appropriate way to respond as a Dental Core Trainee.

options.A: Document the situation appropriately
options.B: Seek advice from a senior colleague
options.C: Ignore the issue
options.D: Communicate clearly with those involved
options.E: Escalate concerns if patient safety may be affected
options.F: Listen carefully and acknowledge the concern

scenario: During a busy clinic session, your educational supervisor tells you that a colleague asks you to sign documentation you did not complete. You must decide how best to respond while maintaining professionalism and ensuring patient safety.

options.A: Reflect on your own practice and identify improvements
options.B: Ignore the issue and continue working as normal
options.C: Seek feedback or clarification from colleagues involved
options.D: Document the concern appropriately
options.E: Discuss the situation with your educational supervisor for guidance
